slices.summary: After a near-miss storm season debrief, Caleb's corridor homeowner workshop series was approved — his first direct leadership role with accountability for outcomes, not just attendance. Discussed designing a checkpoint-based follow-through system, building trust with skeptical homeowners, and locking a pragmatic core strategy before iterating on details.
open_loops: Whether skeptical homeowners' trust in the improved process can be established
Need to finalize with Sharon on participation rate vs attendance as the success standard
Whether first workshop content is simple enough or needs further streamlining
decisions: Lock core framework: sign-in fields, checkpoint definitions, one-page handout, and first follow-up timeline
Align with Miguel on consistent plain-language explanations to build resident trust across sessions
Use post-workshop contact rate and first-document-step completion as early success metrics
